ACKNOWLEDGMENT 
To my dearest wife, Tolulade, 
Your
 
love
 
has
 
been
 
the
 
steady
 
rhythm
 
of
 
my
 
heart,
 
The
 
calm
 
within
 
the
 
storm,
 
the
 
light
 
in
 
my
 
darkest
 
hours.
 
From
 
the
 
moment
 
I
 
felt
 
the
 
whisper
 
to
 
write,
 
You’ve
 
been
 
the
 
spark
 
that
 
fanned
 
the
 
flame.
 
In your quiet strength and unwavering faith, 
I’ve
 
found
 
a
 
partner,
 
a
 
confidant,
 
and
 
a
 
friend.
 
Together,
 
we
 
have
 
walked
 
this
 
journey,
 
hand
 
in
 
hand,
 
And
 
I’ve
 
learned
 
that
 
marriage,
 
at
 
its
 
core,
 
Is
 
not
 
just
 
a
 
union
 
of
 
two
 
hearts,
 
But
 
a
 
partnership
 
of
 
souls
 
intertwined,
 
Each
 
one
 
lifting
 
the
 
other
 
higher,
 
with
 
every
 
step
 
we
 
take.
 
This book is as much yours as it is mine, 
A
 
testament
 
to
 
your
 
love,
 
your
 
sacrifice,
 
your
 
belief
 
in
 
me,
 
And
 
to
 
the
 
beautiful
 
partnership
 
we’ve
 
built,
 
A
 
love
 
that
 
will
 
forever
 
inspire
 
the
 
words
 
within
 
these
 
pages.
 
Thank you for being my muse, my anchor, 
And
 
the
 
irreplaceable
 
jewel
 
in
 
the
 
crown
 
of
 
my
 
life.
 
1

DEDICATION 
To my beloved parents, Dn J.A. Folarin and the late Mrs. F.F. Folarin, 
This book is dedicated to you both, the living embodiment of what a 
perfect
 
marriage
 
looks
 
like.
 
You
 
taught
 
me,
 
not
 
with
 
words
 
but
 
through
 
your
 
daily
 
lives,
 
that
 
love
 
is
 
not
 
just
 
a
 
feeling,
 
but
 
a
 
choice.
 
I grew up witnessing the quiet strength of your union. I never saw you 
argue,
 
nor
 
did
 
I
 
ever
 
see
 
you
 
walk
 
a
 
path
 
apart.
 
Your
 
love
 
was
 
steadfast,
 
unwavering,
 
and
 
pure.
 
You
 
demonstrated
 
what
 
it
 
means
 
to
 
be
 
faithful,
 
not
 
just
 
in
 
word
 
but
 
in
 
action.
 
Your
 
commitment
 
to
 
one
 
another,
 
your
 
unspoken
 
understanding,
 
and
 
your
 
mutual
 
respect
 
formed
 
the
 
very
 
foundation
 
of
 
the
 
ideals
 
I
 
hold
 
dear
 
about
 
marriage.
 
Even in your silence, there was a language of love that spoke volumes. You 
showed
 
me
 
that
 
true
 
partnership
 
is
 
built
 
on
 
shared
 
faith,
 
trust,
 
and
 
the
 
small,
 
everyday
 
acts
 
of
 
care
 
that
 
make
 
love
 
last
 
a
 
lifetime.
 
As I write these words, I remember both of you, your legacy of love and 
faithfulness
 
and
 
I
 
dedicate
 
this
 
work
 
to
 
you.
 
You
 
were,
 
and
 
still
 
are,
 
the
 
perfect
 
example
 
of
 
what
 
a
 
loving
 
and
 
faithful
 
marriage
 
should
 
be.
 
With love and gratitude, 
Your
 
son,
 
 
Daniel Folarin 
 
 
2

PREFACE 
In 2020, I had the honor of being invited by Mrs. Mofifunoluwa Timothy, 
to
 
minister
 
at
 
her
 
marriage
 
meeting.
 
The
 
topic
 
was
 
one
 
that
 
I
 
have
 
always
 
believed
 
in
 
deeply:
 
Emotional
 
and
 
Mental
 
Preparedness
 
for
 
Marriage
.
 
It
 
was
 
during
 
that
 
meeting
 
that
 
the
 
burden
 
to
 
write
 
this
 
book
 
was
 
born.
 
As
 
I
 
shared
 
my
 
thoughts
 
on
 
the
 
emotional
 
and
 
mental
 
readiness
 
required
 
to
 
enter
 
into
 
marriage,
 
I
 
began
 
to
 
realize
 
just
 
how
 
important
 
it
 
is
 
to
 
address
 
these
 
often-overlooked
 
aspects
 
of
 
relationships.
 
Marriage
 
is
 
a
 
lifelong
 
journey,
 
and
 
being
 
mentally
 
and
 
emotionally
 
prepared
 
is
 
essential
 
for
 
navigating
 
its
 
challenges
 
and
 
joys.
 
Since then, I have had the privilege of teaching this topic year after year in 
our
 
church
 
during
 
our
 
marriage
 
emphasis.
 
Each
 
time,
 
I’ve
 
seen
 
the
 
profound
 
impact
 
that
 
these
 
teachings
 
can
 
have
 
on
 
couples,
 
helping
 
them
 
to
 
strengthen
 
their
 
connection
 
and
 
approach
 
marriage
 
with
 
greater
 
clarity
 
and
 
intention.
 
The
 
responses
 
and
 
feedback
 
I
 
received,
 
along
 
with
 
the
 
conviction
 
I
 
felt
 
in
 
my
 
heart,
 
led
 
me
 
to
 
believe
 
that
 
this
 
material
 
could
 
serve
 
a
 
greater
 
purpose.
 
It
 
needed
 
to
 
reach
 
beyond
 
the
 
walls
 
of
 
our
 
church
 
and
 
touch
 
the
 
lives
 
of
 
others
 
who
 
are
 
also
 
seeking
 
wisdom
 
in
 
this
 
important
 
area.
 
Writing this book has been a labor of love, shaped by my experiences, my 
faith,
 
and
 
my
 
desire
 
to
 
help
 
others
 
build
 
strong,
 
resilient
 
marriages.
 
As
 
you
 
read
 
through
 
these
 
pages,
 
I
 
encourage
 
you
 
to
 
approach
 
it
 
not
 
just
 
as
 
a
 
book,
 
but
 
as
 
a
 
guide,
 
a
 
resource
 
to
 
help
 
you
 
reflect,
 
prepare,
 
and
 
grow
 
in
 
your
 
emotional
 
and
 
mental
 
preparedness
 
for
 
the
 
sacred
 
covenant
 
of
 
marriage.
 
This book is for everyone, whether you are preparing for marriage, newly 
married,
 
or
 
have
 
been
 
married
 
for
 
years.
 
The
 
principles
 
shared
 
here
 
are
 
not
 
just
 
for
 
couples
 
in
 
the
 
early
 
stages
 
but
 
for
 
anyone
 
who
 
wishes
 
to
 
strengthen
 
their
 
relationship
 
and
 
cultivate
 
lasting
 
emotional
 
intimacy
 
and
 
understanding.
 
3

I believe that through this material, you will gain a deeper understanding of 
yourself,
 
your
 
partner,
 
and
 
the
 
beautiful
 
journey
 
you
 
are
 
embarking
 
on
 
together.
 
It
 
is
 
my
 
prayer
 
that
 
as
 
you
 
engage
 
with
 
these
 
thoughts
 
and
 
exercises,
 
you
 
will
 
be
 
better
 
equipped
 
to
 
build
 
a
 
marriage
 
that
 
reflects
 
love,
 
respect,
 
and
 
a
 
shared
 
commitment
 
to
 
growth
 
and
 
mutual
 
support.
 
May this book be a blessing to you, and may it lead you to a marriage that 
is
 
rooted
 
in
 
emotional
 
and
 
mental
 
readiness,
 
rich
 
with
 
understanding,
 
and
 
full
 
of
 
grace.
 
4

INTRODUCTION 
Marriage is one of the most profound and transformative relationships we 
experience
 
in
 
life.
 
It
 
is
 
not
 
simply
 
the
 
joining
 
of
 
two
 
people,
 
but
 
the
 
merging
 
of
 
two
 
unique
 
worlds,
 
with
 
all
 
their
 
dreams,
 
desires,
 
values,
 
and
 
even
 
differences.
 
While
 
the
 
journey
 
of
 
marriage
 
is
 
beautiful,
 
it
 
is
 
not
 
always
 
easy.
 
It
 
requires
 
intentionality,
 
growth,
 
and
 
a
 
commitment
 
to
 
not
 
only
 
loving
 
each
 
other,
 
but
 
understanding
 
and
 
adapting
 
to
 
one
 
another.
 
In a society where the ideals of love and commitment often face intense 
pressure,
 
preparing
 
for
 
marriage
 
has
 
never
 
been
 
more
 
important.
 
Whether
 
you
 
are
 
preparing
 
for
 
marriage,
 
newly
 
married,
 
or
 
have
 
been
 
together
 
for
 
years,
 
it
 
is
 
essential
 
to
 
build
 
a
 
solid
 
foundation
 
based
 
on
 
shared
 
values,
 
clear
 
communication,
 
and
 
mutual
 
respect.
 
This
 
book
 
aims
 
to
 
offer
 
practical
 
tools,
 
thoughtful
 
reflections,
 
and
 
insightful
 
questions
 
that
 
guide
 
you
 
through
 
the
 
crucial
 
conversations
 
that
 
lay
 
the
 
groundwork
 
for
 
a
 
successful
 
and
 
lasting
 
marriage.
 
Drawing from the latest research in relationship psychology, wisdom from 
time-tested
 
marriage
 
principles,
 
and
 
biblical
 
truths,
 
this
 
book
 
offers
 
a
 
roadmap
 
for
 
couples
 
to
 
engage
 
in
 
honest
 
and
 
open
 
dialogue
 
about
 
their
 
hopes,
 
dreams,
 
fears,
 
and
 
expectations.
 
It
 
is
 
an
 
invitation
 
to
 
embark
 
on
 
a
 
journey
 
of
 
mutual
 
growth,
 
where
 
both
 
partners
 
can
 
feel
 
heard,
 
understood,
 
and
 
empowered
 
to
 
build
 
a
 
marriage
 
that
 
reflects
 
not
 
just
 
their
 
love
 
for
 
each
 
other,
 
but
 
a
 
shared
 
vision
 
for
 
the
 
future.
 
The conversations you will find within these pages are designed to help 
you
 
both
 
explore
 
the
 
deeper
 
layers
 
of
 
your
 
relationship.
 
From
 
understanding
 
how
 
to
 
navigate
 
challenges
 
to
 
discovering
 
ways
 
to
 
celebrate
 
your
 
unique
 
partnership,
 
this
 
book
 
encourages
 
couples
 
to
 
approach
 
marriage
 
not
 
as
 
a
 
destination,
 
but
 
as
 
a
 
living,
 
breathing
 
commitment,
 
a
 
covenant
 
that
 
thrives
 
with
 
care,
 
effort,
 
and
 
intentionality.
 
5

As you read, consider these reflections not as a checklist, but as a 
framework
 
for
 
cultivating
 
a
 
relationship
 
that
 
grows
 
deeper
 
with
 
each
 
passing
 
year.
 
Marriage
 
is
 
a
 
journey,
 
not
 
a
 
quick
 
fix
 
and
 
it
 
requires
 
dedication,
 
but
 
the
 
rewards
 
are
 
immeasurable.
 
Together,
 
you
 
can
 
build
 
something
 
lasting,
 
meaningful,
 
and
 
beautiful.
 
 
 
 
6

Chapter 1 
WHY MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL PREPAREDNESS MATTERS 
Before you say “I do,” the emotional and mental preparedness that you 
bring
 
into
 
the
 
relationship
 
will
 
largely
 
shape
 
the
 
foundation
 
of
 
your
 
marriage.
 
Love
 
is
 
powerful,
 
but
 
it
 
is
 
not
 
enough
 
to
 
sustain
 
a
 
relationship
 
in
 
the
 
long
 
term.
 
In
 
this
 
chapter,
 
we
 
will
 
explore
 
why
 
mental
 
and
 
emotional
 
preparedness
 
is
 
key
 
to
 
building
 
a
 
strong
 
and
 
lasting
 
marriage.
 
We
 
will
 
define
 
what
 
emotional
 
and
 
mental
 
readiness
 
means,
 
look
 
at
 
why
 
love
 
alone
 
won’t
 
suffice,
 
and
 
explore
 
how
 
to
 
prevent
 
avoidable
 
damage
 
in
 
marriage
 
through
 
proactive
 
emotional
 
intelligence
 
and
 
mental
 
fortitude.
 
When we speak of emotional and mental preparedness for marriage, we are 
referring
 
to
 
the
 
inner
 
tools
 
and
 
qualities
 
that
 
an
 
individual
 
needs
 
to
 
bring
 
into
 
the
 
relationship
 
in
 
order
 
to
 
build
 
a
 
strong,
 
lasting,
 
and
 
harmonious
 
marriage.
 
Both
 
emotional
 
and
 
mental
 
preparedness
 
work
 
in
 
tandem,
 
complementing
 
each
 
other,
 
to
 
form
 
a
 
solid
 
foundation
 
for
 
any
 
relationship.
 
Emotional Preparedness: Recognizing, Understanding, and Managing 
Emotions
 
Emotional preparedness is foundational for creating a safe and nurturing 
environment
 
in
 
a
 
marriage.
 
It’s
 
not
 
simply
 
about
 
“feeling
 
good”
 
all
 
the
 
time.
 
Rather,
 
it’s
 
about
 
the
 
ability
 
to
 
recognize
 
and
 
understand
 
your
 
own
 
emotions,
 
and
 
the
 
emotions
 
of
 
your
 
partner,
 
as
 
well
 
as
 
knowing
 
how
 
to
 
manage
 
them
 
effectively.
 
Let’s
 
break
 
down
 
what
 
this
 
means
 
with
 
specific
 
examples:
 
Recognizing and Understanding Emotions 
Emotional preparedness starts with self-awareness, being aware of your 
feelings
 
and
 
reactions
 
in
 
any
 
given
 
moment.
 
This
 
allows
 
you
 
to
 
engage
 
with
 
your
 
emotions
 
thoughtfully,
 
rather
 
than
 
being
 
controlled
 
by
 
them.
 
7

Example 1: 
Let’s
 
say
 
you
 
come
 
home
 
after
 
a
 
stressful
 
day
 
at
 
work.
 
You
 
feel
 
angry
 
and
 
irritable
 
but
 
can’t
 
figure
 
out
 
why.
 
Being
 
emotionally
 
prepared
 
means
 
recognizing
 
that
 
your
 
irritability
 
isn’t
 
necessarily
 
because
 
of
 
your
 
spouse
 
but
 
rather
 
because
 
of
 
unresolved
 
stress
 
from
 
work.
 
This
 
awareness
 
allows
 
you
 
to
 
take
 
a
 
step
 
back,
 
calm
 
down,
 
and
 
communicate
 
with
 
your
 
spouse
 
about
 
your
 
feelings,
 
instead
 
of
 
lashing
 
out
 
at
 
them.
 
Example 2: 
Your
 
partner
 
gets
 
home
 
late
 
from
 
an
 
event,
 
and
 
you
 
immediately
 
feel
 
upset
 
and
 
neglected.
 
If
 
you
 
are
 
emotionally
 
prepared,
 
you
 
will
 
recognize
 
that
 
your
 
feelings
 
of
 
neglect
 
may
 
be
 
tied
 
to
 
deeper
 
insecurities
 
or
 
fears
 
of
 
abandonment
 
that
 
stem
 
from
 
past
 
experiences.
 
Acknowledging
 
these
 
emotions
 
allows
 
you
 
to
 
discuss
 
them
 
with
 
your
 
partner
 
in
 
a
 
healthy
 
way,
 
without
 
blaming
 
them
 
for
 
the
 
situation.
 
Managing Emotions: Regulating Responses 
Emotional preparedness is not about suppressing your feelings, but rather 
about
 
regulating
 
your
 
emotional
 
responses
 
in
 
a
 
way
 
that
 
doesn’t
 
harm
 
the
 
relationship.
 
You
 
can
 
recognize
 
your
 
emotions
 
without
 
allowing
 
them
 
to
 
control
 
you.
 
This
 
is
 
particularly
 
important
 
in
 
conflict
 
situations,
 
where
 
heightened
 
emotions
 
can
 
often
 
lead
 
to
 
regrettable
 
words
 
or
 
actions.
 
Example 3: 
In
 
an
 
argument,
 
your
 
spouse
 
may
 
say
 
something
 
that
 
triggers
 
old
 
wounds.
 
If
 
you
 
haven’t
 
developed
 
emotional
 
preparedness,
 
you
 
may
 
react
 
defensively,
 
immediately
 
blaming
 
your
 
spouse
 
or
 
becoming
 
withdrawn.
 
However,
 
if
 
you’re
 
emotionally
 
prepared,
 
you
 
will
 
notice
 
the
 
emotional
 
trigger
 
within
 
yourself
 
and
 
take
 
a
 
moment
 
to
 
calm
 
down
 
before
 
responding.
 
This
 
pause
 
allows
 
you
 
to
 
communicate
 
more
 
effectively
 
and
 
without
 
letting
 
past
 
hurts
 
dictate
 
the
 
conversation.
 
8

Empathy and Understanding Your Partner’s Emotions 
Emotional preparedness also means understanding and responding to your 
partner’s
 
emotions.
 
It
 
requires
 
empathy,
 
the
 
ability
 
to
 
step
 
into
 
their
 
shoes
 
and
 
feel
 
what
 
they’re
 
feeling,
 
without
 
dismissing
 
or
 
minimizing
 
their
 
experiences.
 
Example 4: 
Imagine
 
your
 
partner
 
is
 
upset
 
after
 
receiving
 
criticism
 
at
 
work.
 
If
 
you
 
are
 
emotionally
 
prepared,
 
you
 
will
 
not
 
respond
 
with,
 
“It’s
 
no
 
big
 
deal,
 
just
 
forget
 
about
 
it.”
 
Instead,
 
you
 
might
 
say,
 
“I
 
can
 
see
 
how
 
that
 
would
 
hurt
 
your
 
confidence.
 
Do
 
you
 
want
 
to
 
talk
 
about
 
it?”
 
This
 
response
 
shows
 
empathy,
 
allowing
 
your
 
partner
 
to
 
feel
 
understood,
 
which
 
strengthens
 
emotional
 
intimacy
 
in
 
the
 
relationship.
 
Mental Preparedness: The Ability to Think Critically, Set Realistic 
Expectations,
 
and
 
Adapt
 
While emotional preparedness focuses on emotional intelligence and 
managing
 
feelings,
 
mental
 
preparedness
 
refers
 
to
 
the
 
ability
 
to
 
think
 
critically
 
and
 
strategically
 
about
 
your
 
marriage.
 
It
 
involves
 
having
 
realistic
 
expectations,
 
being
 
open
 
to
 
compromise,
 
and
 
understanding
 
that
 
marriage
 
is
 
a
 
dynamic
 
relationship
 
that
 
requires
 
ongoing
 
effort.
 
Mental
 
preparedness
 
equips
 
you
 
to
 
handle
 
life's
 
challenges,
 
adapt
 
to
 
changing
 
circumstances,
 
and
 
prioritize
 
the
 
well-being
 
of
 
the
 
marriage.
 
Setting Realistic Expectations: Understanding What Marriage Truly 
Involves
 
Mental preparedness begins with understanding that marriage isn’t always 
easy
 
or
 
romantic.
 
It’s
 
a
 
commitment
 
to
 
growth,
 
effort,
 
and
 
sometimes
 
discomfort.
 
If
 
you
 
enter
 
marriage
 
with
 
unrealistic
 
expectations,
 
expecting
 
it
 
9

to always be blissful or never difficult, you are setting yourself up for 
disappointment.
 
Example 5: 
Before
 
marriage,
 
you
 
may
 
have
 
envisioned
 
a
 
life
 
where
 
your
 
spouse
 
and
 
you
 
are
 
always
 
in
 
sync,
 
sharing
 
hobbies,
 
agreeing
 
on
 
everything,
 
and
 
living
 
without
 
conflict.
 
However,
 
the
 
reality
 
is
 
that
 
no
 
two
 
people
 
are
 
alike,
 
and
 
differences
 
will
 
naturally
 
arise.
 
Being
 
mentally
 
prepared
 
means
 
acknowledging
 
that
 
you
 
won’t
 
always
 
agree,
 
and
 
that’s
 
okay.
 
You
 
might
 
need
 
to
 
compromise,
 
have
 
difficult
 
conversations,
 
or
 
even
 
agree
 
to
 
disagree.
 
The
 
expectation
 
that
 
marriage
 
will
 
always
 
be
 
easy
 
must
 
be
 
replaced
 
by
 
a
 
more
 
realistic
 
understanding
 
that
 
challenges
 
are
 
a
 
normal
 
part
 
of
 
the
 
journey.
 
Example 6: 
Another
 
unrealistic
 
expectation
 
might
 
be
 
thinking
 
that
 
marriage
 
will
 
instantly
 
make
 
you
 
feel
 
complete.
 
It’s
 
common
 
for
 
people
 
to
 
assume
 
that
 
a
 
spouse
 
will
 
“fill
 
the
 
void”
 
or
 
“fix”
 
their
 
emotional
 
needs.
 
However,
 
mental
 
preparedness
 
involves
 
the
 
understanding
 
that
 
each
 
person
 
must
 
continue
 
to
 
grow
 
individually
 
within
 
the
 
marriage.
 
Expecting
 
your
 
partner
 
to
 
fulfill
 
all
 
your
 
emotional
 
needs
 
can
 
create
 
unnecessary
 
pressure
 
on
 
the
 
relationship.
 
Instead,
 
mental
 
preparedness
 
encourages
 
you
 
to
 
recognize
 
that
 
personal
 
growth
 
and
 
self-awareness
 
are
 
necessary
 
for
 
a
 
healthy
 
relationship.
 
The Importance of Flexibility: Adapting to Change 
Marriage isn’t static, it’s dynamic. Mental preparedness requires the ability 
to
 
adjust
 
when
 
life
 
circumstances
 
change,
 
whether
 
that’s
 
moving
 
to
 
a
 
new
 
city,
 
dealing
 
with
 
job
 
loss,
 
or
 
experiencing
 
health
 
challenges.
 
Example 7: 
You
 
may
 
have
 
entered
 
marriage
 
with
 
the
 
expectation
 
of
 
having
 
children
 
soon
 
after
 
marriage.
 
However,
 
after
 
a
 
couple
 
of
 
years,
 
you
 
and
 
your
 
spouse
 
may
 
decide
 
to
 
delay
 
having
 
kids
 
due
 
to
 
personal
 
goals
 
or
 
career
 
paths.
 10

Being mentally prepared means being flexible and adjusting your plans 
without
 
frustration.
 
It’s
 
about
 
recognizing
 
that
 
your
 
relationship
 
will
 
evolve
 
and
 
being
 
open
 
to
 
change
 
as
 
part
 
of
 
the
 
journey.
 
Example 8: 
In
 
a
 
different
 
scenario,
 
your
 
partner
 
may
 
suddenly
 
lose
 
their
 
job.
 
You
 
might
 
have
 
envisioned
 
a
 
life
 
where
 
both
 
partners
 
contributed
 
equally
 
to
 
the
 
family’s
 
income.
 
Mental
 
preparedness
 
in
 
this
 
situation
 
means
 
you
 
remain
 
flexible,
 
not
 
resentful.
 
You
 
may
 
have
 
to
 
adjust
 
financially,
 
reallocate
 
responsibilities,
 
and
 
provide
 
emotional
 
support
 
to
 
your
 
partner
 
as
 
they
 
navigate
 
the
 
job
 
loss.
 
This
 
flexibility
 
and
 
willingness
 
to
 
adapt
 
show
 
the
 
depth
 
of
 
your
 
commitment
 
to
 
the
 
relationship.
 
Problem-Solving and Adaptation: Handling Life’s Challenges 
Mental preparedness also involves being able to problem-solve when 
issues
 
arise.
 
Conflict
 
is
 
inevitable
 
in
 
any
 
marriage,
 
but
 
your
 
mental
 
capacity
 
to
 
think
 
critically
 
and
 
objectively
 
about
 
the
 
situation
 
will
 
allow
 
you
 
to
 
find
 
solutions
 
rather
 
than
 
getting
 
stuck
 
in
 
frustration.
 
Example 9: 
Imagine
 
that
 
you
 
and
 
your
 
spouse
 
are
 
faced
 
with
 
a
 
significant
 
financial
 
setback.
 
If
 
you
 
are
 
mentally
 
prepared,
 
you
 
won’t
 
panic.
 
Instead,
 
you’ll
 
sit
 
down
 
together
 
to
 
assess
 
the
 
situation,
 
brainstorm
 
solutions,
 
and
 
come
 
up
 
with
 
a
 
plan
 
to
 
move
 
forward.
 
You
 
may
 
need
 
to
 
adjust
 
your
 
lifestyle,
 
cut
 
unnecessary
 
expenses,
 
or
 
take
 
on
 
additional
 
work.
 
The
 
ability
 
to
 
think
 
critically
 
about
 
solutions,
 
without
 
blaming
 
each
 
other,
 
ensures
 
that
 
you
 
both
 
face
 
challenges
 
as
 
a
 
team.
 
 The Synergy Between Emotional and Mental Preparedness 
Emotional and mental preparedness are not separate; they complement and 
enhance
 
one
 
another.
 
Together,
 
they
 
form
 
the
 
core
 
of
 
a
 
marriage
 
that
 
can
 
weather
 
any
 
storm.
 
While
 
emotional
 
preparedness
 
helps
 
you
 
understand
 
11

and manage your feelings and respond empathetically to your partner, 
mental
 
preparedness
 
equips
 
you
 
to
 
think
 
strategically
 
and
 
handle
 
conflicts
 
in
 
a
 
productive
 
way.
 
For example, let’s say that you and your partner have a heated 
disagreement
 
about
 
household
 
responsibilities.
 
Your
 
emotional
 
preparedness
 
allows
 
you
 
to
 
manage
 
your
 
frustration
 
and
 
communicate
 
your
 
feelings
 
without
 
resorting
 
to
 
defensiveness.
